meta.topic: Why I quit Howdy and started Envesty — what I'd do on day 1 now
meta.audience: First-time founders and early-stage startup founders

slides.headline: I co-founded a startup that raised ₹10 Cr. Then it stalled. Here's what I'd do on day 1 now.

slides.subhead: Raised by Howdy Ventures in 2016. The money didn't fix the operational chaos underneath.

slides.headline: What I did then vs. what I'd do now

left.label: Howdy (2016)
left.body: 4 vendors. 2 lawyers. 1 CA. No integration. Each had a different answer on the same question. Every week was a coordination sprint.

right.label: Envesty (2025)
right.body: One team. One timeline. One phone number. Legal, tech, and marketing move together — or they flag each other before they block the founder.

slides.headline: What actually kills early startups — what I saw vs. what I believed

left.label: What I believed
left.body: Wrong product. Wrong market. Wrong timing. The usual startup autopsy.

right.label: What I saw
right.body: Compliance backlogs that froze fundraising. Vendor gaps no one owned. A founding team spending 30% of their week chasing status updates from four different people.

slides.headline: The most expensive thing in your first year isn't your tech stack. It's the coordination cost of 5 vendors who don't talk to each other.
What's your day 1 situation?
slides.body: DM me your situation — I'll share the exact setup I'd build if I were starting in India in 2025. No pitch.

caption:
I co-founded a startup that raised ₹10 Cr. Then it stalled.

Here's what I'd do on day 1 now.

Howdy Ventures was real. The raise was real. What I underestimated was the operational layer underneath the product.

4 vendors. 2 lawyers. 1 CA. No one talking to each other.

Every week had the same pattern:
→ Chase the CA for a compliance status
→ Chase the lawyer for a contract
→ Chase the dev agency for a timeline
→ Chase everyone to talk to each other

The product wasn't wrong. The coordination was broken.

That's why I built Envesty — one accountable team for the legal, tech, and marketing layer a founder actually needs to move.

We're 16 months old. We don't have 100 clients. We have a small, real team and a process that works.

If you're starting a Pvt Ltd in India this year — DM me. I'll share the setup I'd build on day 1.

No pitch. Just the actual answer.

#Founder #IndiaStartup #StartupIndia #Entrepreneurship #PvtLtd #FounderStory
hooks: My first startup raised ₹10 Cr and still failed. Here's the mistake I didn't see coming.
The most expensive line item in a founder's first year is one nobody budgets for.
I didn't start Envesty because incorporation is broken. I started it because coordination is.
cta: DM me — I'll send the timeline I built for the last founder in your situation.
